visualizerParams.title: Millionth Lexicographic Permutation
visualizerParams.problemDescription: Refactor the permutation calculation using a mathematical approach (factorial number system) for near-instant results.

solution.code:
import math

def solution(n: int = 1000000, digits: str = "0123456789") -> str:
    """
    Finds the n-th permutation using the factoradic (factorial number system).
    This avoids generating any permutations at all.
    """
    res = ""
    candidates = list(digits)
    # Convert n to 0-indexed
    n -= 1
    
    for i in range(len(digits) - 1, -1, -1):
        fact = math.factorial(i)
        idx = n // fact
        res += candidates.pop(idx)
        n %= fact
        
    return res


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(solution(1000000))
solution.explanation: The mathematical approach using the factorial number system (factoradic) is O(k^2) where k is the number of digits, making it vastly faster and more efficient than iterating through 1,000,000 permutations.
